transcript: Hey everyone, I'm Solomon, I'm uh, a product engineer um at the Solana Foundation, and I'm super excited to bring to you Day 2 uh at the Dev Stage. This is my third Breakpoint. It's been really great to walk around and see so many new faces, so many familiar ones, and to see all of these ecosystem activations around the venue. There's some really solid ones that are very fun, unique and quirky, so please make sure to go through the entire venue and see everything that's happening around. Um, for today I wanted to do a quick intro on why we're here in the first place and then go through some logistics for the rest of the day. So, a year prior we used to have a lot of developer focused events called Hacker Houses. Hacker Houses were these uh, sort of event venues uh maintained by the Solana Foundation to allow developers from across the world to congregate in certain areas to dive deep into certain technical topics and applications. And unfortunately, um, hackathons do exist, but Hacker Houses in their old form do not. And we got a lot of feedback from many developers in the ecosystem basically saying, hey, we really want more serendipity, we want more of these events to talk about what we actually care about, which are these deep technical topics that typically you won't find at these events where it's mainly product, marketing, sales and BD. And so we thought really hard, uh we didn't want to basically re- uh recreate what we had in the past, but we wanted to make sure that no matter where we're showing up across the world, that we would still have these events that are tailored towards the audience that they they should be for. And so uh beginning of the year, in May, we actually had the first um version of a new type of event called Accelerate. Accelerate was uh during May in in in sunny New York, and we basically wanted to basically re- recreate a a new type of event that was at the time mostly focused on finance, policy and regulation, but we also wanted to have a separate event primarily for tech. And so we had this week-long event, um and at the beginning we had a very specific event for the developers which was called Scale or Die. And Scale or Die was was my baby, it was it was a really uh awesome event. Um and a lot of people had really really great things to say. The outcomes were very clear. We wanted all the developers um in in one place to dive deep into really important ideas across core dev, across security, across infrastructure, and uh we did exactly that. We actually got a lot of um really really rave reviews. Developers and teams would come up to me and say, hey, this was this was one of the best events I've ever been to because they were able to congregate around specific topics that only matter to them. And so, um as a result we were like, okay, well, we should have dev- developer events at every venue that we're showing up at. And so here, um at the Dev Day at Breakpoint, we wanted to basically re- reinvigorate the developer community and make sure that we had talking points and um and uh, you know, an event for for you guys. And so today we're going to have a lot of really awesome talks across security, across infrastructure. We're going to have a really interesting block with with Firedancer called One Firedancer where uh they'll talk about, you know, Firedancer uh on mainnet and and accruing more stake. We'll have Anza have their own block of talks uh primarily focused on performance and and reliability at all costs. And then we'll have some things like Anchor um and 1.0 and some other pieces across the stage. So, I'm really excited to to bring uh the Dev Day to you. For logistics, uh all talks today are actually only 10 minutes or 15 minute uh talks. Uh usually at Dev Stages we try not to do fireside chats or panels. We want to get deep into the actual content and make sure that people are learning net new things and are sort of getting uh new information that they usually wouldn't have. Um, it's available for everyone, it'll be livestreamed and recorded, and so I'm really excited to have uh this developer focused day to make sure that we're all reinvigorating the tech and next year we'll focus on developers and distribution. Thank you.

enrichment.generated_query: Scale or Die Solana Foundation Solana Breakpoint 2025 (announcement OR blog OR documentation OR docs)

sources.title: Onchain x LI.FI: Solana Breakpoint Campaign
sources.snippet:
Get Started on Onchain Wallet

[Download Now](https://crypto.com/en/onchain)

[EVENTS](https://crypto.com/en/events) \|2D AGO

# Onchain x LI.FI: Solana Breakpoint Campaign

Win USDC rewards or exclusive Onchain merch

10 - 17 DEC, 2025UTC

To mark Solana Breakpoint 2025, Crypto.com Onchain has partnered with Li.Fi for a special week-long campaign featuring an in-person giveaway and a Solana on-chain trading challenge.

Whether you're attending Breakpoint in Abu Dhabi or participating online, there’s something for everyone! Complete two simple activities to claim exclusive merchandise or trade using Onchain to win from a 2,000 USDC prize pool.

**CAMPAIGN-SPECIFIC TERMS:**

**Campaign Period**

- **Activity 1: Meet & Selfie Challenge:** 10 December 2025, 00:00 UTC to 13 December 2025, 23:59 UTC
- **Activity 2: Solana Trading Challenge:** 10 December 2025, 00:00 UTC to 16 December 2025, 23:59 UTC

**Activity 1. Meet & Selfie (@Breakpoint)**

Join us at the Etihad Arena in Abu Dhabi! Meet the Onchain or Li.Fi team, complete a simple trade, and walk away with exclusive merchandise.

**How to Participate**

_If you wish to participate in the campaign, you may consider completing the following actions:_

- Find an Onchain Wallet or LI.FI team member at Breakpoint,
- Take a selfie with them,
- Post the selfie publicly on X (formerly Twitter),
 - Tag: @onchain\_wallet and @lifiprotocol
 - Include hashtag: #LIFIOnchain
- Download and open the Crypto.com Onchain Wallet, and
- Complete a trade using LI.FI in the Crypto Onchain Wallet app
 - Any chain, any amount, cross-chain or same-chain

**Reward**

- Choose from Onchain-branded swag (“Merch Reward”).
- Available to the first 40 verified and eligible users during the Campaign Period of the Activity 1.
- Merch Reward must be claimed on-site after staff confirmation.
- Limit of one Merch reward per user per day.

**Activity 2: Solana Trading Challenge**

Trade using Onchain Wallet’s LI.FI integration with Solana as part of the route. The top three traders by volume during the campaign will win USDC rewards.

**How to Participate**

_If you wish to participate in the campaign, you may consider completing the following actions:_

- Use Onchain Wallet’s LI.FI-powered swap feature
- Trade involving Solana
 - Examples: ETH to SOL, SOL to USDC, USDC on Solana
- No minimum amount required
- Only trades completed using LI.FI inside the Onchain Wallet will count
- All trades must be performed on Onchain Wallet within the Campaign Period of the Activity 2.

**Rewards**

- **1st Place:** 1,000 USDC
- **2nd Place:** 500 USDC
- **3rd Place:** 250 USDC
- **4th Place:** 150 USDC
- **5th place:** 100 USDC

Winners will be announced within 7 days after the Campaign Period of the Activity 2 ends. Rewards will be sent directly to the eligible wallet addresses on Solana within 7 days after the winner announcement of Activity 2.

**Eligibility and Rules**

- No signup required. Wallet activity and public posts are used to determine eligibility
- All actions must take place within the campaign windows listed above
- Each user is only eligible for one reward per track per day
- Suspicious activity such as wash trading or duplicate wallets will lead to disqualification
- In case of a tie in trading volume, the user who reached that volume first will win
- Onchain Wallet staff will verify on-site winners by checking trade history and social media posts
- All rewards are subject to verification and may be withheld if conditions are not met

### **Important Information**

- The Crypto.com Onchain Wallet is a standalone, non-custodial product and is not part of the regulated Crypto.com App or Exchange ecosystem.
- The Solana Breakpoint Campaign (“ **Campaign**”) is organised by Crypto.com Onchain Wallet and LI.FI, and is [available in select jurisdictions only](https://help.crypto.com/en/articles/4429907-crypto-com-onchain-wallet-swap-geo-restrictions). Participation is optional and subject to eligibility requirem

tags.impact_reason: A recap of developer-focused events and a preview of Dev Day at Breakpoint, indicating an effort to engage and support the developer community but lacking immediate, tangible impact.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a futuristic, high-performance engine core as the Semantic Anchor because the story is about the Solana Foundation's intense pivot to a 'Scale or Die' engineering ethos. The engine represents immense power, technical complexity, and the critical need for performance and reliability mentioned in the transcript (Firedancer, network reliability). I included the text 'SCALE // DIE' as a holographic diagnostic alert on the machine itself, making it a diegetic part of this high-stakes environment and directly reflecting the core message from the headline.
visual_idea.description: A macro, detailed shot of the engine core's surface. The frame is filled with the intricate junction where several thick, glowing fiber optic cables, pulsing with magenta and cyan light, plug into a port on the polished tungsten casing. The metal's surface reflects the intense energy, creating complex caustic light patterns and revealing micro-etched engineering details. No text is visible.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A macro, detailed shot of an engine core's surface, meticulously composed for a 1:1 square aspect ratio. The frame is entirely filled with the intricate junction where several thick, glowing fiber optic cables, pulsing intensely with vibrant magenta and cyan light, plug into a precisely engineered port on the highly polished tungsten casing. The lustrous metal surface reflects the intense energy of the cables, creating mesmerizing, complex caustic light patterns that ripple and dance across the casing. Volumetric lighting effects, emanating from the glowing cables, cast a soft atmospheric glow and illuminate every deeply micro-etched engineering detail and fine texture on the metallic surface, showcasing exquisite craftsmanship and mechanical precision. The composition emphasizes symmetry and central focus, with no text visible. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, cinematic volumetric lighting, studio lighting, high contrast.

editorial.headline: Solana Foundation Pivots to 'Scale or Die' Engineering Ethos at Breakpoint
editorial.standfirst: Product Lead Solomon Ponomarev unveils a strategic shift from generalist Hacker Houses to high-intensity technical summits. The new 'Dev Day' format prioritizes network reliability and client diversity, headlined by Firedancer's mainnet expansion and Anza's performance roadmap.
key_takeaways: Firedancer Accruing Stake: The Jump Crypto-backed validator client is live on mainnet and actively increasing its stake weight to boost network resilience.
Anza's Reliability Mandate: The core development team is debuting a 'reliability at all costs' roadmap, moving beyond feature velocity to stability.
Anchor 1.0 Release: The dominant Solana development framework is hitting its 1.0 milestone, signaling API maturity for institutional builders.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
The era of the generalist 'Hacker House' is effectively over. In a sharp strategic pivot, **Solana Foundation** Product Engineer Solomon Ponomarev announced that the protocol's developer events are moving toward a 'Scale or Die' philosophy—a rigorous, engineering-first approach designed to mature the ecosystem for institutional scale. This shift comes in direct response to developer feedback demanding 'serendipity' and deep technical discourse over the marketing-heavy activations that have historically characterized crypto conferences.

This new direction is not merely cosmetic; it signals a fundamental maturation of the Solana infrastructure stack. The 'Dev Day' agenda prioritizes high-signal technical briefings over panels and fireside chats, with a clear focus on network stability. The headline development is the **Firedancer** client, which is now confirmed to be on mainnet and actively 'accruing more stake.' For institutional investors, the successful deployment and adoption of a second, independent validator client is a critical risk-mitigation milestone, eliminating the network's single point of failure.

Complementing this is the evolved roadmap from **Anza**, the core development shop spun out of Solana Labs. Ponomarev highlighted Anza's new mandate of 'performance and reliability at all costs,' a significant tonal shift from the 'move fast and break things' ethos of previous cycles. Coupled with the announcement of **Anchor 1.0**—marking the stability of the ecosystem's primary development framework—Solana is signaling to the market that its rails are hardening. The focus has decisively shifted from user acquisition to engineering resilience, ensuring the network can support the next generation of high-throughput financial applications.
